title: TUI _log_row_from_map may expose internal dict structure in message
description:
File: python/src/lichen/tui/native.py
Lines: 1016-1020

The _log_row_from_map function falls back to the entire dict if no message key found:

    def _log_row_from_map(item: Mapping[str, Any]) -> LogRow:
        level = safe_display_value("level", item.get("level", item.get("severity", "info")))
        module = safe_display_value("module", item.get("module", item.get("source", "device")))
        message = safe_display_value("message", item.get("message", item.get("msg", item)))
                                                                              ^^^^
                                                                              Falls back to entire dict

If a log entry lacks both 'message' and 'msg' keys, the entire dict is passed to safe_display_value, which renders it as:
    "key1=value1, key2=value2, ..."

This could expose internal structure including potentially sensitive fields that weren't intended for display, even though safe_display_value does redact known sensitive keys.

Impact: Minor information disclosure if log format is unusual.

Fix: Use a default like '--' or 'no message' instead of the raw dict:
    message = safe_display_value("message", item.get("message", item.get("msg", "--")))
close_reason: Fixed fallback in _log_row_from_map to use '--' default instead of raw dict to prevent potential internal structure exposure. Tests, ruff, and mypy (pre-existing errors only) pass. Commit ae3432ff2
